title: Mishandling of characters in VHDL parameters
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.55+23 (git sha1 e57a2b944, ccache clang++ 18.1.8 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

string.vhd
```vhdl
library ieee;
use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
use ieee.numeric_std.all;

entity top is
  port (
    a : out character
  );
end entity;

architecture rtl of top is
component sub is
  generic (
    s : character
  );
  port (
    a : out character
  );
end component;
begin
  sub_i: sub
    generic map (
        s => '1'
    )
    port map (
        a => a
    );
end architecture;
```
sub.vhd
```vhdl
entity sub is
  generic (
    s : character := 'a'
  );
  port (
    a : out character
  );
end entity;

architecture rtl of sub is
begin
  a <= s;
end architecture;
```
Run `yosys string.vhd sub.vhd -p 'hierarchy -top top'`

### Expected Behavior

Yosys completes normally, as it does when there is no generic and `a <= '1';` directly.

### Actual Behavior

```
VERIFIC-INFO [VHDL-1067] string.vhd:5: processing 'top(rtl)'
VERIFIC-INFO [VHDL-1067] sub.vhd:1: processing '\sub(s='1')\(rtl)'
Importing module top.
Importing module \\sub(s='1')\(rtl).
ERROR: Error parsing VHDL character.
```

This appears to be coming from the following function: https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/2223d7848be5a249b885c355904aa1142081ea23/frontends/verific/verific.cc#L273-L278

It seems that character extraction assumes that characters start and end with double quotes.  Most characters start and end with single quotes rather than double quotes, but some don't have quotes at all.  They're also only ever 8bits rather than 32.  Having a wire of type `character`, we also see this in the resulting RTLIL:
```
  attribute \enum_value_11111111 "\\'\377'"
  attribute \enum_value_11111110 "\\'\376'"
...
  attribute \enum_value_10100000 "\\'\240'"
  attribute \enum_value_10011111 "\\c159"
  attribute \enum_value_10011110 "\\c158"
...
  attribute \enum_value_10000000 "\\c128"
  attribute \enum_value_01111111 "\\del"
  attribute \enum_value_01111110 "\\'~'"
  attribute \enum_value_01111101 "\\'}'"
  attribute \enum_value_01111100 "\\'|'"
  attribute \enum_value_01111011 "\\'{'"
  attribute \enum_value_01111010 "\\'z'"
  attribute \enum_value_01111001 "\\'y'"
...
  attribute \enum_value_01100001 "\\'a'"
  attribute \enum_value_01100000 "\\'`'"
  attribute \enum_value_01011111 "\\'_'"
  attribute \enum_value_01011110 "\\'^'"
  attribute \enum_value_01011101 "\\']'"
  attribute \enum_value_01011100 "\\'\\'"
  attribute \enum_value_01011011 "\\'['"
  attribute \enum_value_01011010 "\\'Z'"
  attribute \enum_value_01011001 "\\'Y'"
...
  attribute \enum_value_01000001 "\\'A'"
  attribute \enum_value_01000000 "\\'@'"
  attribute \enum_value_00111111 "\\'?'"
  attribute \enum_value_00111110 "\\'>'"
  attribute \enum_value_00111101 "\\'='"
  attribute \enum_value_00111100 "\\'<'"
  attribute \enum_value_00111011 "\\';'"
  attribute \enum_value_00111010 "\\':'"
  attribute \enum_value_00111001 "\\'9'"
  attribute \enum_value_00111000 "\\'8'"
...
  attribute \enum_value_00110000 "\\'0'"
  attribute \enum_value_00101111 "\\'/'"
  attribute \enum_value_00101110 "\\'.'"
  attribute \enum_value_00101101 "\\'-'"
  attribute \enum_value_00101100 "\\','"
  attribute \enum_value_00101011 "\\'+'"
  attribute \enum_value_00101010 "\\'*'"
  attribute \enum_value_00101001 "\\')'"
  attribute \enum_value_00101000 "\\'('"
  attribute \enum_value_00100111 "\\'''"
  attribute \enum_value_00100110 "\\'&'"
  attribute \enum_value_00100101 "\\'%'"
  attribute \enum_value_00100100 "\\'$'"
  attribute \enum_value_00100011 "\\'#'"
  attribute \enum_value_00100010 "\\'\"'"
  attribute \enum_value_00100001 "\\'!'"
  attribute \enum_value_00100000 "\\' '"
  attribute \enum_value_00011111 "\\usp"
  attribute \enum_value_00011110 "\\rsp"
...
  attribute \enum_value_00000000 "\\nul"
  attribute \wiretype "\\character"
  attribute \src "string.vhd:7.5-7.6"
  wire width 8 output 1 \a
```

which implies that Yosys is already getting a map of 8bit values to their character representation.  Maybe we can use that somehow?
